instruction:
We have data on the lean body mass and resting metabolic rate for 12 women who are subjects in a study of dieting. Lean body mass, given in kilograms, is a person's weight leaving out all fat. Metabolic rate, in calories burned per 24 hours, is the rate at which the body consumes energy.
$\begin{array}{ccccccccccccc}\hline Mass & 50.3 & 47.8 & 41.7 & 46.8 & 43 & 48.4 & 45.6 & 43.5 & 45.8 & 39 & 45.7 & 44.7 \\ \hline Rate & 1310 & 1410 & 1130 & 1300 & 1250 & 1090 & 1200 & 1200 & 990 & 1170 & 1450 & 1120 \\ \hline \end{array}$
Find the least-squares regression line for predicting metabolic rate from body mass. ANSWER: $\hat{y}=$ [ANS]
